dataclasses/InitVar.name: InitVar
dataclasses/InitVar.description: InitVar[T] type annotations describe variables that are init-only. Fields annotated with InitVar are considered pseudo-fields, and thus are neither returned by the fields() function nor used in any way except adding them as parameters to __init__() and an optional __post_init__().

dataclasses/is_dataclass.name: is_dataclass
dataclasses/is_dataclass.description: Return True if its parameter is a dataclass (including subclasses of a dataclass) or an instance of one, otherwise return False. If you need to know if a class is an instance of a dataclass (and not a dataclass itself), then add a further check for not isinstance(obj, type): def is_dataclass_instance(obj): return is_dataclass(obj) and not isinstance(obj, type)
